fraud_indicators.name: New Policy Syndrome
fraud_indicators.description: Policy issued within 90 days before the claim date. High-risk window where pre-existing damage is disguised as a new incident.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Verify policy inception date vs incident date. Check if any pre-inception survey was conducted. Confirm full premium was paid upfront rather than installments.

fraud_indicators.name: Lapse-Reinstate Before Claim
fraud_indicators.description: Policy was lapsed for more than 30 days and then reinstated within 60 days of the claim being filed. Suggests calculated reinstatement before staging an incident.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Request full payment history and reinstatement records. Check gap period and any vehicle usage during lapse.

fraud_indicators.name: Simultaneous Multi-Policy
fraud_indicators.description: Multiple active policies on the same vehicle from different insurers. Enables double or triple-dipping on the same claim.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Cross-check with IIB (Insurance Information Bureau). Contact other insurer to verify.

fraud_indicators.name: Serial Claimant
fraud_indicators.description: Policyholder has filed 3 or more claims in the past 24 months on any vehicle or policy. Statistically anomalous claim frequency.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Pull complete claim history across all policies. Look for pattern in location, workshop, or incident type.

fraud_indicators.name: Pre-Renewal Claim Spike
fraud_indicators.description: Claim filed within 30 days of policy renewal to inflate no-claim discount base or secure renewal.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Check policy renewal date. Verify FIR timestamp against claim filing time.

fraud_indicators.name: Late Reporting Delay
fraud_indicators.description: Incident reported more than 72 hours after occurrence without medical or logistical justification. Creates time for evidence manipulation.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Request written explanation for delay. Compare with any CCTV or FIR timestamps available.

fraud_indicators.name: Pre-existing Damage in Photos
fraud_indicators.description: Rust, faded paint, prior unrepaired dents, or worn parts visible in submitted photos indicate damage preceded the incident date.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Compare with any pre-inception survey photos. Request independent garage inspection report. Check VAHAN records for prior accidents.

fraud_indicators.name: Physics Mismatch
fraud_indicators.description: Damage location or pattern physically impossible given the described collision type, speed, and direction. E.g., right-side damage claimed from left-side impact.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Engage accident reconstruction expert. Request FIR with spot sketch if available. Check for CCTV at incident location.

fraud_indicators.name: Selective High-Value Component Damage
fraud_indicators.description: Expensive components (engine, transmission, airbags) claimed as damaged without proportionate visible body damage that would cause such internal damage.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Request detailed workshop inspection by empanelled surveyor. Verify airbag crash data recorder if available.

fraud_indicators.name: Symmetrical Damage Anomaly
fraud_indicators.description: Perfectly symmetrical damage on both sides of vehicle inconsistent with any standard collision scenario. Often indicates deliberate vandalism or staged damage.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Request site inspection. Look for tool marks vs impact deformation patterns.

fraud_indicators.name: Remote Isolated Location
fraud_indicators.description: Accident reported in remote, unmanned area with no witnesses, no CCTV, and no other traffic. Provides no verification pathway.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Check vehicle telematics/GPS if available. Verify with local police station for any reports from that area.

fraud_indicators.name: Weather Claim Contradiction
fraud_indicators.description: Flood, hail, or weather-related claim but official meteorological records show no such event at claimed location on claimed date.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Cross-check IMD (India Meteorological Department) data. Check OpenWeatherMap historical records for the exact date and coordinates.

fraud_indicators.name: Grossly Inflated Workshop Estimate
fraud_indicators.description: Repair estimate from workshop exceeds market OEM rate by more than 50% without justification. Suggests workshop collusion.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Get second independent estimate. Check if workshop is on insurer's approved list. Verify parts availability and pricing via MAIA database.

fraud_indicators.name: OEM Parts Claimed on Old Vehicle
fraud_indicators.description: OEM parts claimed for vehicle older than 7 years where aftermarket parts are standard and OEM availability is limited.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Verify parts availability from OEM dealer. Apply depreciation schedule per IRDAI guidelines.

fraud_indicators.name: Insistent on Specific Workshop
fraud_indicators.description: Claimant refuses alternate workshops and insists on one specific garage not on insurer's approved panel.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Investigate relationship between claimant and workshop. Request cashless authorization only at approved garage.

fraud_indicators.name: Excessive Familiarity with Process
fraud_indicators.description: Claimant demonstrates unusual knowledge of claim procedures, exact document requirements, and settlement timelines — more than expected for a first-time claimant.
fraud_indicators.investigation_action: Check if claimant or family member works in insurance industry. Review claim history.

known_fraud_schemes.name: New Policy Pre-Existing Damage Fraud
known_fraud_schemes.description: Vehicle already damaged before policy inception. Policy taken out expressly to claim for damage that existed before coverage. Most common in comprehensive policies taken within 60 days of claim.
common_indicators: Policy age < 60 days
No pre-inception survey
Rust or weathering visible on damaged parts
Damage inconsistent with described incident speed
known_fraud_schemes.case_example: HIST-010 — Renault Kwid, rust visible on headlights, policy 12 days old, rejected

known_fraud_schemes.name: Staged Collision
known_fraud_schemes.description: Two cooperating parties deliberately arrange a collision, typically in low-traffic areas with no witnesses. Both file claims with their respective insurers, or one files against the other's third-party cover.
common_indicators: No independent witnesses
Both parties from same locality
Minor visible damage but large repair claim
No police FIR despite significant damage
Both vehicles insured by same company
known_fraud_schemes.case_example: HIST-012 — VW Polo staged collision with vehicle from same locality, rejected

known_fraud_schemes.name: Serial Theft or Total Loss Fraud
known_fraud_schemes.description: Vehicle sold for parts or to a third party but reported as stolen or total-loss accident. FASTag and VAHAN records often expose vehicle movement post-reported-theft.
common_indicators: Multiple prior theft/total-loss claims
Vehicle cannot be produced for inspection
FIR in wrong jurisdiction
FASTag data shows vehicle movement after reported theft
known_fraud_schemes.case_example: HIST-007 — Tata Safari, FASTag showed vehicle in Rajasthan after Bangalore theft claim

known_fraud_schemes.name: Workshop Inflation Conspiracy
known_fraud_schemes.description: Genuine minor accident used as opportunity to inflate repair estimates significantly. Workshop and claimant share the excess settlement. Common in non-cashless settlements.
common_indicators: Workshop not on approved panel
Estimate 2x+ market rate
Claimant demands reimbursement not cashless
Same workshop used across multiple claims
known_fraud_schemes.case_example: HIST-006 — Honda Amaze, estimate 40% above market, settled at reduced amount

known_fraud_schemes.name: Weather Claim Fabrication
known_fraud_schemes.description: Claimant falsely reports vehicle damaged in hail, flood, or storm when no such event occurred at the claimed location and date. Weather data from IMD or OpenWeatherMap directly contradicts the claim.
common_indicators: No weather event recorded at location
Damage inconsistent with claimed weather type
Claim filed days after supposed weather event
known_fraud_schemes.case_example: HIST-004 — Hyundai Creta flood claim, no rainfall on date, pre-existing engine issues, rejected

medium_risk.label: Medium
medium_risk.action: Enhanced manual review required before settlement

critical.label: Critical
critical.action: Reject pending SIU investigation and legal review
